name: Rincon
parking_notes: Rincon Beach County Park lot. Fills fast on good swells. Street parking along PCH also available.

name: Ventura Point (C Street)
parking_notes: Free street parking along Promenade. Larger lot at Surfer's Knoll nearby. Get there early on weekends.

name: Mondos (Mussel Shoals)
parking_notes: Small gravel pullout along PCH. Limited spots — arrive early. Overflow parking on shoulder.

name: County Line
parking_notes: Free dirt lot on both sides of PCH at the LA/Ventura county line. Can get crowded on weekends.

name: Zuma Beach
parking_notes: Large LA County beach lot. $3/hr, $25 max. Kiosks and pay-by-plate. Free after 7pm.

name: Malibu (Surfrider Beach)
parking_notes: Malibu Lagoon State Beach lot. Very limited — arrive before 8am on good days. Street parking on PCH fills fast.

name: Topanga
parking_notes: Topanga State Beach lot. Day use fee collected seasonally. Can park on PCH shoulder for free (check signs).

name: El Porto
parking_notes: Metered street parking on Highland Ave and side streets. Meters run 8am–6pm. Free on street after hours.

name: Manhattan Beach Pier
parking_notes: Meters on Manhattan Beach Blvd and The Strand. Lots on Valley Dr. Gets crowded on summer weekends.

name: Hermosa Beach
parking_notes: Street meters on Pier Ave and Hermosa Ave. City lots nearby. 2-hr limit on many blocks — plan accordingly.

name: Torrance Beach (RAT Beach)
parking_notes: Free street parking along Paseo de la Playa. Walk-in access from the bluff stairs. Limited spots.

name: Huntington Beach Pier
parking_notes: Large city lot and meters on PCH. $2/hr, $20/day max. State Beach lot adjacent charges $15/day on weekends.

name: Huntington Cliffs
parking_notes: Free street parking on PCH north of the pier. Walk down the sandy path to the beach.

name: 56th Street Newport
parking_notes: Free street parking on Balboa Peninsula side streets. Meters active on Balboa Blvd — check signs.

name: Newport Beach Pier
parking_notes: City lots and meters near the pier. $2/hr. Lot on 21st St. Gets packed in summer — arrive early.

name: Blackies
parking_notes: Free street parking along W Balboa Blvd near 19th St. Walk to the beach. Local vibe — keep it chill.

name: The Wedge
parking_notes: Free street parking on Peninsula Point. Limited spots — can fill by 7am on big days. Walk from Balboa Park.

name: Salt Creek
parking_notes: Salt Creek Beach Park lot. $1/hr metered, $15 max. Stairs down to beach. Fills fast on south swells.

name: Dana Point (Doheny State Beach)
parking_notes: Doheny State Beach day use fee. Large paved lot right on the sand. Great for families.

name: T-Street (San Clemente)
parking_notes: Free street parking on Buena Vista and T Street. Limited spots. Arrive early on summer mornings.

name: San Clemente Pier
parking_notes: City Beach lot and meters near the pier. $2/hr. Walk the pier and watch the lineup before paddling out.

name: Cottons (San Clemente)
parking_notes: Park at the Trestles trailhead (Carl's Jr lot on Cristianitos Rd). ~20-min walk to Cottons.

name: Uppers (Trestles)
parking_notes: Park at the Trestles trailhead (Carl's Jr lot on Cristianitos Rd). ~25-min walk in through San Onofre State Beach.

name: Lowers (Trestles)
parking_notes: Park at the Trestles trailhead (Carl's Jr lot on Cristianitos Rd). ~25-min walk in. Day use pass sometimes enforced.

name: Middles (Trestles)
parking_notes: Park at the Trestles trailhead (Carl's Jr lot on Cristianitos Rd). Between Uppers and Lowers on the walk in.

name: San Onofre (Old Man's)
parking_notes: San Onofre State Beach day use fee. Vehicle entry required. Chill longboard vibe with fire pits.

name: Oceanside Pier
parking_notes: City beach lot at the pier. $2/hr. Large structure — easy to find. Street parking also available nearby.

name: Oceanside Harbor
parking_notes: Free parking in the harbor area. Jetty break — wave depends on sand build-up. Consistent and fun on good swells.

name: Cardiff Reef
parking_notes: San Elijo State Beach lot. Day use fee. Walk-in access also from the road shoulder — watch for fast traffic on PCH.

name: Swamis (Self-Realization Fellowship Reef)
parking_notes: Free street parking on S. Coast Hwy 101. Stairs down the bluff. Arrive early — very popular spot.

name: Pipes (Cardiff)
parking_notes: San Elijo State Beach south lot. Reef break just south of Cardiff Reef. Often less crowded than Swamis.

name: Del Mar (15th Street)
parking_notes: City meters on Coast Blvd and side streets. $2/hr. Walk to the beach from 15th St access. Nice beach town vibe.

name: Black's Beach
parking_notes: Park at Torrey Pines Gliderport or Torrey Pines State Reserve ($15/day). Steep cliff trail to the beach — use caution.

name: La Jolla Shores
parking_notes: Free street parking on Calle Frescota and Camino del Oro. Fills fast on weekends — arrive by 8am.

name: Windansea
parking_notes: Free street parking on Neptune Place. Very limited — expect to park several blocks away and walk.

name: Pacific Beach (Tourmaline)
parking_notes: Free parking lot at Tourmaline Surfing Park. No swimming allowed — dedicated surf spot. Fills fast on mornings.

name: PB Pier (Pacific Beach)
parking_notes: Meters on Ocean Blvd and Grand Ave. City lot on Pacific Beach Dr. Gets crowded year-round — PB is party central.

name: Mission Beach
parking_notes: Limited street parking on Mission Blvd. Lot at Belmont Park charges $10+. Walk or bike when possible.

name: Ocean Beach Pier
parking_notes: Free city lot at Niagara Ave and Abbott St. Street parking on Newport Ave. OB has a local surfer culture.

name: Sunset Cliffs
parking_notes: Free street parking on Sunset Cliffs Blvd. Multiple entry points along the cliffs — scout at low tide. Rocky entries.

name: Imperial Beach Pier
parking_notes: Free street parking and small lot near the pier. IB is the southernmost surf spot before the border. Consistent beach break.
